motion: Should PR #28: epic: Frontend Scaffold & Integration be merged into main?

findings.description: selectTable error overwrites global error state, destroying working UI on single table load failure
findings.fix_applied: Scoped to tableError variable; global error only set by onMount. Inline banner for table-level failures.

findings.description: Error state permanent after transient failure — error never cleared on recovery
findings.fix_applied: tableError is cleared at start of selectTable (line 42). Global error is never set by selectTable.

findings.description: Pagination permanently disabled — only page 1 ever loads for multi-page datasets
findings.rationale: Scaffold PR scope — all interactive buttons (search, columns, export, pagination) are intentionally disabled placeholders. Backend pagination is fully implemented. Wiring is Epic 3.
findings.arbiter_ruling: Reclassified from blocker to HIGH follow-up. Disabled placeholders are consistent with scaffold scope.

findings.description: Export button wired to nothing despite fully implemented backend
findings.rationale: Same scaffold pattern as pagination — placeholder button

findings.description: Duplicate table-list nav markup across error and normal branches
Mock generates only 50 rows, masking pagination gap during mock development
No loading state indicator during initial data fetch
Sidebar localStorage restore causes flash of expanded-to-collapsed on mount

fixes_applied: Finding 1+2: selectTable error handling scoped to tableError variable (commit 8a75543)
follow_up_issues: Wire pagination in StatusBar (finding 3 — HIGH priority, Epic 3)
Extract TableList.svelte to deduplicate nav markup (finding 5)
